articles.title: Cumhurbaşkanı Erdoğan'dan İran açıklaması. "Savaşı durdurmak için çalışıyoruz, önceliğimiz ateşkesin sağlanması"
articles.title_tr: Cumhurbaşkanı Erdoğan'dan İran açıklaması. "Savaşı durdurmak için çalışıyoruz, önceliğimiz ateşkesin sağlanması"
articles.summary:
Cumhurbaşkanı Recep Tayyip Erdoğan, AK Parti Ankara teşkilatına verilen iftar programında açıklamalarda bulundu. Cumhurbaşkanı Erdoğan İran'a yönelik saldırıların uluslararası hukuku açıkça ihlal ettiğini söyledi. Erdoğan, 
"Savaşı durdurmak için çalışıyoruz. Önceliğimiz ateşkesin sağlanması, diyalog kapısının açılmasıdır" ifadelerini kullandı.
articles.summary_tr:
Cumhurbaşkanı Recep Tayyip Erdoğan, AK Parti Ankara teşkilatına verilen iftar programında açıklamalarda bulundu. Cumhurbaşkanı Erdoğan İran'a yönelik saldırıların uluslararası hukuku açıkça ihlal ettiğini söyledi. Erdoğan, 
"Savaşı durdurmak için çalışıyoruz. Önceliğimiz ateşkesin sağlanması, diyalog kapısının açılmasıdır" ifadelerini kullandı.
articles.long_summary:
Cumhurbaşkanı Recep Tayyip Erdoğan, AK Parti Ankara teşkilatına verilen iftar programında açıklamalarda bulundu. Cumhurbaşkanı Erdoğan İran'a yönelik saldırıların uluslararası hukuku açıkça ihlal ettiğini söyledi. Erdoğan, 
"Savaşı durdurmak için çalışıyoruz. Önceliğimiz ateşkesin sağlanması, diyalog kapısının açılmasıdır" ifadelerini kullandı.
articles.long_summary_tr:
Cumhurbaşkanı Recep Tayyip Erdoğan, AK Parti Ankara teşkilatına verilen iftar programında açıklamalarda bulundu. Cumhurbaşkanı Erdoğan İran'a yönelik saldırıların uluslararası hukuku açıkça ihlal ettiğini söyledi. Erdoğan,

articles.title: Young cancer survivors face faster aging and possible early dementia
articles.title_tr: Young cancer survivors face faster aging and possible early dementia
articles.summary: Surviving cancer at a young age may come with an unexpected cost: faster aging at both the cellular and brain levels. Researchers found that survivors often show signs of being biologically older than their actual age, with chemotherapy accelerating the process most dramatically. This accelerated aging is linked to struggles with memory and focus, which can ripple into education and career outcomes. Encouragingly, scientists believe healthy habits like exercise may help turn back the clock.
articles.summary_tr: Surviving cancer at a young age may come with an unexpected cost: faster aging at both the cellular and brain levels. Researchers found that survivors often show signs of being biologically older than their actual age, with chemotherapy accelerating the process most dramatically. This accelerated aging is linked to struggles with memory and focus, which can ripple into education and career outcomes. Encouragingly, scientists believe healthy habits like exercise may help turn back the clock.
articles.long_summary: Surviving cancer at a young age may come with an unexpected cost: faster aging at both the cellular and brain levels. This accelerated aging is linked to struggles with memory and focus, which can ripple into education and career outcomes. Encouragingly, scientists believe healthy habits like exercise may help turn back the clock.
articles.long_summary_tr: Surviving cancer at a young age may come with an unexpected cost: faster aging at both the cellular and brain levels. articles.title: A bold new plan could finally cure type 1 diabetes
articles.title_tr: A bold new plan could finally cure type 1 diabetes
articles.summary: Researchers are developing a two-part therapy for type 1 diabetes: lab-made insulin-producing cells paired with custom-engineered immune cells that protect them. The goal is to stop the immune system from destroying transplanted cells — without using immunosuppressive drugs. Backed by $1 million in funding, the team hopes to create a ready-to-use treatment that could work even for people who have had diabetes for years. The approach could transform how the disease is treated.
articles.summary_tr: Researchers are developing a two-part therapy for type 1 diabetes: lab-made insulin-producing cells paired with custom-engineered immune cells that protect them. The goal is to stop the immune system from destroying transplanted cells — without using immunosuppressive drugs. Backed by $1 million in funding, the team hopes to create a ready-to-use treatment that could work even for people who have had diabetes for years. The approach could transform how the disease is treated.

Backed by $1 million in funding, the team hopes to create a ready-to-use treatment that could work even for people who have had diabetes for years. The approach could transform how the disease is treated.
articles.long_summary_tr: Researchers are developing a two-part therapy for type 1 diabetes: lab-made insulin-producing cells paired with custom-engineered immune cells that protect them. The goal is to stop the immune system from destroying transplanted cells — without using immunosuppressive drugs. Backed by $1 million in funding, the team hopes to create a ready-to-use treatment that could work even for people who have had diabetes for years. The approach could transform how the disease is treated.

articles.title: Paramount Insists HBO Will 'Operate With Independence' After Warner Bros. Buyout, Though Streaming Service Will be Combined With Paramount+
articles.title_tr: Paramount Insists HBO Will 'Operate With Independence' After Warner Bros. Buyout, Though Streaming Service Will be Combined With Paramount+
